text.title: Domain Layer: Entities, Value Objects, and Domain Events
text.summary: BaseEntity with domain event collection, rich entity with static factory and behavior methods, and Value Object with factory validation.

rule.statement: Domain entity constructors must be private; expose behavior via static factory methods (User.Create(...)) that raise domain events. Never new-up entities outside their aggregate.
rule.rationale: Private constructors prevent agents from bypassing invariant checks. Static factories enforce all required fields and ensure domain events are raised consistently on creation.

rule.statement: Value objects for every primitive concept (Email, Money, Slug); never pass raw strings across aggregate boundaries.
rule.rationale: Stringly-typed arguments let agents pass emails where URLs are expected. Value objects enforce format invariants at construction and make type mismatches a compile error.
